point_reviews.reason: The document states that COVID-19 spreads mainly from person to person through respiratory droplets.
The document confirms that the virus can be transmitted when an infected person coughs, sneezes, or talks.
The document mentions that close contact with an infected person increases the risk of transmission.
The document states that COVID-19 can spread in areas with low airflow, increasing transmission risk.
The document confirms that people can spread the virus even if they do not develop symptoms.
The document states that touching surfaces contaminated with the virus and then touching the face can lead to transmission.

The document states that COVID-19 is mainly transmitted through inhaling air contaminated by droplets and aerosols.
The document confirms that infected individuals exhale particles when they breathe, talk, cough, sneeze, or sing.
The document states that transmission is most likely at close range but can occur over longer distances, especially indoors.
The document mentions that COVID-19 can spread when infectious particles come into contact with the eyes, nose, or mouth.
The document states that small airborne particles can remain suspended in the air and travel longer distances indoors.
The document confirms that touching surfaces contaminated with the virus and then touching the face can lead to transmission.
The document states that people can spread the virus even if they do not develop symptoms, remaining contagious for up to 20 days.
The document states that COVID-19 spreads mainly from person to person through respiratory droplets.
The document confirms that the virus can spread from an infected person's mouth or nose in small droplets when they cough, sneeze, or breathe.
The document states that people can be contagious whether or not they are displaying symptoms.
The document mentions that infection can occur by touching the mouth, nose, or eyes after touching contaminated surfaces.
The document states that spending time in crowded spaces with poor airflow raises the risk of catching COVID-19.
